tasks.title: BitReader.fill() accumulates nbits past data end, risking u32 overflow on pathologically large images
tasks.defer_reason: The overflow mechanism is real: nbits is u32 and is never reset between rows, so feeding phantom 0-bytes past EOF accumulates it unboundedly. However, the scenario requires approximately 536M/16 â‰ˆ 33M phantom fill calls, which would demand a declared image of ~33M pixels with a near-empty stream â€” at the practical edge of 32-bit WASM address space (4 GB). The finding correctly describes the arithmetic (u32 wrap â†’ subtraction underflow â†’ shift modulo 64 in release) but the exploitation preconditions are extreme enough that real-world impact is negligible. Downgraded from medium to low in effective severity.

tasks.title: width * height multiplication can silently overflow on 32-bit WASM, making the assert pass with wrong dimensions and enabling unsafe get_unchecked OOB reads.

tasks.title: width == 0 or height == 0 causes usize underflow on (width - 1) and (height - 1), producing isize::MAX as w_max/h_max.
tasks.reason_for_tier: workalone mode: all tasks at sonnet

tasks.title: get_unchecked in demosaic `at()` is safe for normal images but the assert does not prevent stride-overflow OOB on wasm32
tasks.defer_reason: demosaic.rs lines 11-14 confirmed. The `assert_eq!(raw.len(), width * height)` at line 27 does overflow-match `decompress`'s `vec![0u16; width * height]`, so on wasm32 a wrapped allocation means the assert passes and `get_unchecked` aliasing stays within bounds. However, the caller (lib.rs line 259) only reaches demosaic after checking `width != 0 && height != 0` (line 108), but there is no cap on width/height values from the file, so the overflow scenario is real for crafted files. The finding is real but correctly self-assessed as 'no true OOB in current code' â€” the risk is future-regression fragility of the unsafe block.

tasks.title: at() uses get_unchecked with coordinates computed through isize arithmetic; an overflow in the calling dimension setup would produce out-of-bounds unsafe access.
tasks.defer_reason: The `unsafe get_unchecked` at demosaic.rs:13 is real. The safety argument depends on whether `width == 0` or `height == 0` can reach `demosaic_rggb`. The check at tiff.rs:108 (`if info.width == 0 || info.height == 0`) causes `parse()` to return an `Err`, so zero-dimension inputs are rejected before reaching `demosaic_rggb`. However, `demosaic_rggb` has a public API that does not itself guard against `width == 0`; the `assert_eq!(raw.len(), width * height)` at line 27 would not catch width=0/height=0 if both are zero (0==0 passes). If width=0 reaches the function, `w_max = (0usize - 1) as isize` wraps to `isize::MAX` on wasm32, and the clamp bounds become nonsensical, making `get_unchecked` truly unsafe. Exploitability depends on whether any call path can pass width=0 given current guards â€” it appears blocked by the tiff parser, making this uncertain for the existing call paths.
